questions.question: Good relationship is an important factor that helps a group to function effectively.
options: True
False

questions.question: Communication originates from the Latin word "communis", which means "to make common."
options: True
False

questions.question: Which option best challenges the statement "hard skills are measurable and soft skills are not"?
options: Hard skills can be graded using tests, while soft skills cannot be observed.
Soft skills can be assessed through behavior-based rubrics, though not with absolute precision
Soft skills are personal traits and therefore irrelevant in professional settings
Hard skills are learned, while soft skills are inherited

questions.question: ___________________ is a two-way process, and there is always a direct link between the quality of our communication and the quality of our life.
options: Communication
Good relationship
Presentation skills
None of the above

questions.question: Which statement MOST accurately reflects the role of words and non-words in communication?
options: Words convey meaning, while non-words only add emotion and are optional
Non-words always communicate more accurately than words
Words transmit explicit messages, while non-words often modify, reinforce, or contradict them
Words and non-words function independently and never affect each other

questions.question: In Fisher's model of group progression, in which stage do the group members get to know one another and come up with the problems they have convened to deal with?
options: Orientation
Conflicts
Emergence
Reinforcement

questions.question: Read the following statements and choose the correct option. Statement 1: The status of a communicator can influence how a message is interpreted by the receiver. Statement 2: Messages from higher-status individuals are often accepted with less questioning.
options: Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are true, and Statement 2 is the correct explanation of Statement 1
Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are true, but Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1
Statement 1 is true, but Statement 2 is false
Statement 1 is false, but Statement 2 is true

questions.question: Which kind of communicators believe that the primary purpose of communication is the maintenance or advancement of the personal relationship?
options: Socratic communicator
Noble communicator
Reflective communicator
None of the above

questions.question: From the perspective of concrete (pattern) poetry, which statement BEST explains the communicative function of "Easter Wings"?
options: The poem's meaning depends entirely on metaphor, not on its visual form
The poem uses imagery only through figurative language, not through physical arrangement
The visual layout distracts the reader from the poem's religious message
The wing-shaped structure visually enacts the themes of fall and redemption, reinforcing verbal meaning

questions.question: An effective group occurs when the members have ___________________ and recognize that their personal success is dependent on the success of others.
options: similar jobs
a common goal
a common work environment
the same leader

questions.question: In communication, images always function as supportive elements that clarify the meaning of the accompanying text.
options: True
False

questions.question: Read the following statements and choose the correct option. Statement 1: Increasing the spatial or conceptual distance between an image and its accompanying text can reduce their perceived interdependence in communication. Statement 2: Close integration of image and text ensures complete elimination of ambiguity in meaning.
options: Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are true, and Statement 2 is the correct explanation of Statement 1
Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are true, but Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1
Statement 1 is true, but Statement 2 is false
Statement 1 is false, but Statement 2 is true

questions.question: A presenter may shorten or lengthen a presentation based on real-time audience reaction and engagement, demonstrating effective ___________________.
options: feedback management
encoding
noise control
channel selection

questions.question: Which principle BEST guides the effective use of text size and amount in a presentation?
options: Slides should contain as much text as possible to avoid verbal repetition
Smaller text allows more information to be displayed without distracting the audience
Larger text with limited content enhances readability and supports spoken explanation
Text size and amount are irrelevant if visuals are used

questions.question: In which situation should a communicator AVOID conveying a strong sense of enthusiasm?
options: When motivating a team toward a challenging goal
When presenting a creative proposal to potential investors
When introducing a new initiative to employees
When delivering sensitive or distressing information
